item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used for this article is not compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0), as the source is from here. If you'd like to contact the artist of the original image and ask them for permission to use the image, please do and provide evidence of the exchange and we will add the image back. However, with the age of the image, this is unlikely. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really interesting idea here. Has some really disturbing possible effects, for being a safe SCP no less. This reminds me of the plot of "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind", seems to be a similar effect.

item.title: Relatable
item.content:encoded: Having married in one of those in Vegas not even half a year ago made this extra… unnerving. Skip starts out as a "Oh, this one is actually almost beneficial" and quickly segues to nightmare territory. Good idea, good writing. 1+ from me. Would hug wife but she's asleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what form that’d take, maybe there’s a tale there. Romancing Larry: Viva Las Vegas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m more so trying to imply that existing long-lasting relationships are less susceptible to its effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1, but maybe rephrase the statement about the effects deterioration for clarity. "less-acute" isn't a commonly used term.

Also, isn't it a bit contradictory that the effect is permanent yet it loses strength over time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe if you chilled with 106 at the location it make all the torture seem like happy times?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 I like the idea of your's

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd rather play with 106 than go here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a lot better than the first draft for sure. You found a way to make the deteriorating marriages a part of the description that made sense rather than just going off on a tangent. And you kept the ending line, which was my personal favorite part.

Good stuff, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That’s what I always shoot for in these kind of articles. Putting up a frame that people can fill in themselves with what scares them. Nothing I could write here would be as unnerving as what you could come up with in your own head.

I’m pleased that you found it thought-provoking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's some very low-key horror here about the possible implications of this that really sell it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally written for the Short works contest, I've had it on ice until I figured out a good way to retool it. Hopefully people find this to be interesting.

Image credit to Faminepulse. Thanks to unclenicolini, Gishface, OthelloTheCat, weryllium and everyone else who helped this come into existence.

unfinished draft present on this page when I went to post
unfinished being the key word

Item #: SCP-4326

Object Class: Euclid keter

Special Containment Procedures: at the time of this report there is no way to fully contain SCP-4326, the longest lasting tactic is too encase SCP-4326 in a vinyl case and to administer 220 volts of electricity every 3 hours.

emergency breakout procedures: in the event of SCP-4326 breaking out of containment all SCPs containing mechanical parts are to be put on immediate lock down if failed to do so then all D-class personell will be used as shields.

description
